evals.prompt: I have a Node.js Express API that uses CommonJS require() throughout. I need to migrate it to ESM modules. The project also needs to work as a library consumed by both ESM and CJS users. Show me how to restructure the package.json, update the module imports, and set up dual-format exports.
evals.expected_output: A migration plan with package.json exports field configuration for dual ESM/CJS, updated import statements, and guidance on handling __dirname/__filename replacements in ESM.
assertions: Sets "type": "module" in package.json or explains the ESM entry point strategy
Configures the "exports" field with both "import" and "require" conditions for dual-format support
Replaces require() calls with import statements and explains __dirname replacement using import.meta.url
Addresses barrel exports and tree-shaking implications for the library surface
Includes or references testing the dual-format output to verify both CJS and ESM consumers work

evals.prompt: I'm building a data processing service that needs to run on both Node.js and Deno. It fetches paginated data from a REST API, transforms each page, and writes results to a file. I need it to handle cancellation (user presses Ctrl+C), retry transient failures, and limit concurrency to 5 parallel requests. Show me how to write this cross-runtime.
evals.expected_output: A cross-runtime implementation using fetch(), AbortSignal for cancellation, async generators for pagination, a concurrency limiter, and conditional runtime-specific file I/O.
assertions: Uses AbortController/AbortSignal for cancellation with signal propagation through the fetch chain
Implements retry logic with exponential backoff that respects the abort signal
Limits concurrent requests to 5 using a semaphore or queue pattern rather than unbounded Promise.all
Uses runtime detection or conditional imports for file system operations (node:fs vs Deno.writeFile)
Uses async generators or ReadableStream for paginated data processing instead of loading all pages into memory
